paper.title_en: Gravitational Memory Beyond Null Infinity through Finite-Distance Carrollian Screens
paper.abstract_en: We investigate gravitational memory beyond null infinity by studying finite-distance null hypersurfaces endowed with Carrollian geometry. We show that the intrinsic degenerate geometry, optical data, and null Brown--York tensor of a finite null screen define a quasilocal Carrollian dissipative system, providing a natural framework to characterize the residual geometric response after the passage of radiation. To make this construction explicit and compare it with the standard asymptotic description, we use Robinson--Trautman spacetimes as an exactly solvable radiative setting. For asymptotically flat Robinson--Trautman geometries, we transform the solution to Bondi gauge and extract the asymptotic data directly in terms of the Robinson--Trautman field. In the linearized sector, the Bondi shear is purely electric and produces the standard displacement-memory effect associated with the relaxation toward the final Schwarzschild geometry. We show that the leading large-radius tracefree component of the finite-screen memory reduces to the Bondi displacement memory, while finite-distance corrections retain additional focusing, embedding dependence, angular drift, Coulombic data, and near-zone information. Thus, Bondi memory emerges as the universal asymptotic projection of a broader quasilocal Carrollian response. Late-time screens approaching the final Schwarzschild horizon exhibit exponentially decaying non-isotropic Carrollian data, leaving only the isotropic null Brown--York stress. As a by-product, we construct the Robinson--Trautman solution with nonzero cosmological constant to second order in the radiative amplitude and use the holographic dictionary to study the associated energy fluxes and find that the resulting charge does not obey a universal monotonicity property.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文通过Carrollian几何研究有限距离处的引力记忆效应，将Bondi位移记忆推广为准局域响应，理论基础扎实，方法新颖清晰。
decision.title_zh: 超越零无穷远的引力记忆：通过有限距离的Carrollian屏
decision.abstract_zh: 我们通过研究具有Carrollian几何的有限距离零超曲面，探讨了超越零无穷远的引力记忆。我们证明，有限零屏的内在退化几何、光学数据和零Brown-York张量定义了一个准局域的Carrollian耗散系统，这为刻画辐射通过后的残余几何响应提供了一个自然框架。为了使这一构造明确并与标准渐近描述进行比较，我们使用Robinson-Trautman时空作为可精确求解的辐射环境。对于渐近平坦的Robinson-Trautman几何，我们将解变换到Bondi规范，并直接根据Robinson-Trautman场提取渐近数据。在线性化区域，Bondi剪切是纯电型的，并产生与最终Schwarzschild几何弛豫相关的标准位移记忆效应。我们证明，有限屏记忆的领先大半径无迹分量简化为Bondi位移记忆，而有限距离修正保留了额外的聚焦、嵌入依赖性、角漂移、Coulomb型数据和近区信息。因此，Bondi记忆作为更广泛的准局域Carrollian响应的普适渐近投影出现。接近最终Schwarzschild视界的晚期屏展现出指数衰减的非各向同性Carrollian数据，仅剩下各向同性的零Brown-York应力。作为副产品，我们在辐射振幅的二阶构造了具有非零宇宙常数的Robinson-Trautman解，并利用全息字典研究了相关的能量通量，发现所得电荷不满足普适的单调性性质。
